> **DQJCY** — "The recent contraction is a demonstrably liquidity-driven event completely divorced from DQJCY's exceptional fundamental performance, rendering it a high-conviction mean-reversion opportunity once the technical stop-loss parameters are properly tightened to protect capital."

🟡 **MLSPF (BUY_LIMIT)** has no DR verdict yet — possibly still in the 60s-rate-limited queue, or skipped. Worth checking before tomorrow's open.

## 6. Broken-service / data-health flags

| Severity | Flag | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| 🔴 **HIGH** | `decision_tracking` is **completely empty** | 0 rows for 573 decisions. The post-decision price-tracker is not writing. This breaks every backtest, win-rate-by-recommendation report, and the planned multi-week evaluation extension. Suspect tracker worker has been silently dead since inception or fails to insert. |
| 🔴 **HIGH** | `ai_score` is NULL on **563/573 (98.3%)** rows | Including all of today's 13. Either the scorer was removed from the pipeline, or the column was renamed and writes are landing elsewhere. Downstream ranking that joins on `ai_score` will be using a sparse signal. |
| 🔴 **HIGH** | `sector` is NULL on **565/573 (98.6%)** rows | Sector enrichment is not running. Affects sector-cluster summaries, sector-rotation analysis, and the audit-report grouping in §5 (manual cluster substituted). |
| 🔴 **HIGH** | **20 `decision_points` flagged `status='Owned'` but have no `desk_position`** | Orphaned "Owned" rows: BJ, EDU, WMT, NU, PODD, UI, SYM, plus three TEST rows (TEST, TEST_T3, TEST_T12) and others. The two state tables are drifting — either positions were closed without back-writing `decision_points.status`, or test fixtures were never cleaned. |
| 🔴 **HIGH** | **DR funnel coverage on BUY_LIMIT = 34.8%** (23/66) | 43 of 66 BUY_LIMIT rows lifetime never received a DR verdict. Includes 13 rows from 5/11 onwards (MLSPF, GKOS, XP, RDDT, SOLS, ADI, ARGTF, APPF, APP, ELAN, VIV, FLUT, plus a few owned-status BUY_LIMITs). BUY coverage is fine (94%) so this is a BUY_LIMIT-specific routing bug. |
| 🟠 **MEDIUM** | **All 5 active positions last reviewed 2026-05-14** | 12-day staleness. The position review loop has not run since 5/14. This is also why **WTW is sitting below its stop** with no action recorded. |
| 🟠 **MEDIUM** | **WTW current_price < stop_loss_price** | $247.72 vs $253.50 SL — should have triggered a STOP_LOSS exit. Either the price feed is stale on the position row (`current_price` last refreshed pre-stop) or the exit job is stuck. |
| 🟠 **MEDIUM** | Today's **PASS_INSUFFICIENT_DATA rate 7/13 (54%)** vs lifetime 11% | Almost all are non-US ADRs (CHGCY, BP, TIGO, CRCL, MOH, RBLX, TSCO). Either ADR data pull is broken today, or the data-depth threshold tightened. Worth a single look. |
| 🟠 **MEDIUM** | MHVIY desk_position is ACTIVE but `decision_points.status='Not Owned'` | Inverse of the orphan issue above. Same root cause (state drift between tables). |
| 🟡 **LOW** | 3 lifetime `ERROR_PARSING` DR cases | HQY, PPERF, ADBE — all >5 weeks old. Failure was forced-AVOID which is the safe default. Not blocking. |
| 🟡 **LOW** | Subscribers table is empty | If you intentionally have no mailing list yet, ignore. If email summaries are supposed to be going out, this is wrong. |
| 🟡 **LOW** | TEST rows in production DB | IDs 367, 368, 375 with symbols TEST / TEST_T3 / TEST_T12 are still in `decision_points`. Cleanup nit. |

---

## 7. Insights & takeaways

- **Strategy is still net-positive but the loss tail is getting fatter.** Closed P&L sum +44.3% across 40 positions is solid, but five of the last ten closed trades hit stop-loss or trailing stop with losses of −5.9% to −9.2%. The TP-heavy April cohort (8 names, all >6.5% gains) is what's carrying the curve; May closures are roughly flat-to-down. Worth investigating whether the gatekeeper or Phase 1/2 agents have drifted since the regime check tightened.
- **DR is doing its job on BUY rows — and is the right defensive layer.** 25 of 67 BUY → DR cases (37%) were overridden to AVOID. The PM by itself would have been net-too-bullish; DR is the load-bearing risk filter and it's worth protecting (rate limit, prompt stability).
- **…but DR is bypassing 65% of BUY_LIMIT rows.** That's the biggest functional bug in the pipeline right now. BUY_LIMIT recs are the ones most likely to convert into live positions later (as price drifts into the entry range), so an unscreened BUY_LIMIT can become a live trade without DR ever weighing in. Either the DR queue isn't enqueueing BUY_LIMIT cleanly, or the worker filters them out.
- **Decision tracking is unusable.** `decision_tracking` has 0 rows lifetime, which means every claim about "how the pipeline's recommendations have actually performed over the following N days" is currently un-falsifiable. This is the precondition for the backlog's #1 item (backtesting harness).
- **Position-management cadence has stopped.** No `desk_positions` review since 5/14, and WTW is below its stop with no exit recorded. The dip-buying side is fine; the position-management side has visibly drifted.
- **Today's signal volume was light and ADR-heavy.** 13 candidates, of which 7 were dropped for insufficient data and 5 of those 7 are non-US ADRs. Net actionable BUY count: 2 (AZO, DQJCY), both DR-confirmed at unchanged levels. Neither has been added to positions yet.
- **Subscribers = 0 is either intentional or a silent regression.** Worth checking once whether email summaries should be going somewhere.
- **Small-sample noise to discount, not absorb:** today's −29% volume vs baseline is one weekday after a 3-day weekend — well within normal Tuesday-after-holiday range. Don't read into it.

---

## 8. Suggested next actions (prioritised by impact)

1. **Fix `decision_tracking` writes (BLOCKER for backtesting).** Find the tracker worker (likely in `app/services/tracking_service.py`), confirm it's running in startup, and verify it actually `INSERT`s. Until this is fixed every accuracy metric is anecdotal.
2. **Resolve the BUY_LIMIT DR funnel gap.** Inspect `app/services/deep_research_service.py` enqueue logic — confirm BUY_LIMIT verdicts are added to the same priority queue as BUY. As a one-off, batch-enqueue the 43 un-DR'd BUY_LIMIT rows (run the existing `run_deep_research.sh` against them) so the backlog catches up.
3. **Restart / debug the desk_positions review loop and re-run today.** Five positions stale 12 days, one of them already through its stop. Manually re-review WTW first and decide whether to honor the stop or roll it.
4. **Restore `ai_score` and `sector` enrichment.** 98%+ NULL on both columns means two ingestion jobs are silently failing. Pick whichever is easier to verify (sector via FinnHub/Polygon profile) and confirm the call still works.
5. **Reconcile `decision_points.status` vs `desk_positions`.** Write a one-shot script to (a) unflag the 20 orphaned "Owned" rows that have no live position and (b) flip MHVIY's `decision_points.status` to "Owned". Drop the 3 TEST_ rows while you're in there.
6. **Decide AZO and DQJCY before tomorrow's open.** Both are DR-confirmed BUYs at unchanged levels, with the only outstanding question being whether AZO clears the position-size limit at $3,100/share.
7. **Chase the missing DR on MLSPF.** If the DR worker has actually processed it but the write failed, you'll see it in logs; if it's still queued, it'll fire on the next cycle. Either way, MLSPF shouldn't sit overnight without a verdict.
8. **One-pass on the ADR data pipeline.** Seven of 13 today's PASS_INSUFFICIENT_DATA was foreign tickers — check whether the data-depth threshold or the foreign-ticker resolver shifted in the last week.
